title: Frontend Developer Job Simulation
description: Ship realistic frontend tasks: build reusable components, debug broken flows, integrate APIs, and deploy a working mini-feature.
about: This simulation mirrors a junior frontend engineer's sprint in a startup. You will design small components, fix real bugs, and submit production-style links.
modules: Bug fixing in a broken codebase

tasks.title: Build a Responsive Navbar
tasks.description: Create a responsive navbar with desktop and mobile states. Include keyboard-friendly toggle behavior and active link highlighting.
tasks.deliverable: Public repository link with README and at least one screenshot of mobile + desktop view.

tasks.title: Reusable Card Component
tasks.description: Build a React card component that supports props for title, summary, image, tags, and CTA. Add one loading state and one empty state.
tasks.deliverable: Repository or sandbox link with reusable component and example usage in 3 variations.

tasks.title: API Integration with Pagination
tasks.description: Fetch users from JSONPlaceholder, render a paginated list, and handle loading/error/empty states. Add search by name.
tasks.deliverable: Deployed demo URL plus source repo link. Both links should be public.

tasks.title: Bug Hunt: 5 Production Bugs
tasks.description: Fix 5 intentional bugs in routing, state updates, and form validation. Document each bug, root cause, and final fix.
tasks.deliverable: Repository commit history or PR showing fixes plus a short bug report document.

tasks.title: Release Notes
tasks.description: Write concise release notes summarizing features, known limitations, and next sprint improvements.
tasks.deliverable: Public document with release notes and roadmap items.

faq.a: No. It is self-paced and project-driven.

faq.q: Do I need to submit code?
faq.a: Yes. Most tasks expect a public repo, sandbox, or deployed link.

faq.q: What do I get for INR 99?
faq.a: A verified PDF certificate with role and QR verification after completing tasks.

faq.q: Can I use my own stack?
faq.a: Yes, as long as your submission proves the required functionality.

title: Data Analyst Job Simulation
description: Run a realistic analytics workflow: clean data, analyze trends, visualize results, and present business recommendations.
about: You will work like an entry-level analyst handling a noisy dataset and presenting decisions to stakeholders.

tasks.title: Dataset Quality Fix
tasks.description: Clean null values, remove duplicates, standardize date/currency formats, and create a quality report.
tasks.deliverable: Notebook or repo with before/after stats and cleaned dataset link.

tasks.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
tasks.description: Identify top trends and anomalies. Add summary statistics and at least 3 investigative questions.
tasks.deliverable: Notebook/report showing findings with reasoning and assumptions.

tasks.title: Visualization Pack
tasks.description: Create 4 visualizations (trend, distribution, comparison, and correlation). Include chart captions.
tasks.deliverable: Dashboard or report with visuals and business interpretation.

tasks.title: SQL Business Queries
tasks.description: Write 5 SQL queries for business questions (top customers, retention, revenue trend, etc.).
tasks.deliverable: SQL file and outputs/screenshots with short interpretation.

tasks.title: Executive Summary
tasks.description: Prepare a one-page summary with risks, opportunities, and prioritized recommendations.
tasks.deliverable: Public document with non-technical summary for decision makers.

faq.a: Python, SQL, Excel, or BI tools are all accepted.
No. Validation checks your link quality and completion flow.

faq.q: What do I get for INR 99?
faq.a: A verified downloadable PDF certificate with your role.

title: UI/UX Designer Job Simulation
description: Go from research to interactive prototype with practical UX deliverables.
about: A realistic design sprint where you define user pain points, map flows, and craft a clickable prototype.

tasks.title: User Research Brief
tasks.description: Define target users, goals, pain points, and success metrics for a food delivery product.
tasks.deliverable: Research notes with 2 personas and journey highlights.

tasks.title: Low-Fidelity Wireframes
tasks.description: Design wireframes for Home, Search, Product Detail, Cart, and Checkout screens.
tasks.deliverable: Wireframe file with navigation flow and annotations.

tasks.title: High-Fidelity Prototype
tasks.description: Build an interactive prototype with components, typography scale, and color tokens.
tasks.deliverable: Public prototype link with design system page.

tasks.title: Usability Findings
tasks.description: Run a quick usability review and document at least 5 issues with fixes and priority.
tasks.deliverable: Issue log with severity labels and improvement proposal.

faq.q: Do I need prior Figma experience?
faq.a: Basic knowledge helps, but beginner-level workflows are acceptable.

faq.q: Can I use Adobe XD or Canva?
faq.a: Yes, but your submission must include interaction flow and rationale.

faq.q: What do I get for INR 99?
faq.a: A verified PDF certificate with role and completion details.

title: Backend Developer Job Simulation
description: Design, implement, secure, and test a production-style backend service with realistic engineering tasks.
about: This simulation reflects backend sprint work: API contract design, implementation, authentication, debugging, and test reliability.

tasks.title: API Contract (OpenAPI)
tasks.description: Design an OpenAPI spec for a task-management backend with auth, CRUD, pagination, and error responses.
tasks.deliverable: Public OpenAPI file or Postman documentation with sample request/response payloads.

tasks.title: Implement Core Endpoints
tasks.description: Build user/task CRUD with Express + Mongoose. Include validation and proper status codes.
tasks.deliverable: Public repository with setup instructions and environment variables guide.

tasks.title: Authentication + Authorization
tasks.description: Add register/login, JWT token verification, and role-based protection for at least one admin route.
tasks.deliverable: Repository update plus a short auth flow document.

tasks.title: Production Bug Fix Pack
tasks.description: Fix 3 intentional backend bugs: one auth bug, one data validation bug, and one async/await bug.
tasks.deliverable: PR or commit history showing bug fixes with before/after explanation.

tasks.title: API Test Suite
tasks.description: Add Jest + Supertest tests for at least 4 endpoints including one edge case each.
tasks.deliverable: Test report or CI run link proving tests pass.

faq.q: Why am I not asked for Figma here?
faq.a: Backend tasks are validated against backend/documentation/testing platforms, not UI design tools.
Node.js + Express + MongoDB is recommended, but equivalent backend stacks are acceptable.

faq.q: What do I get for INR 99?
faq.a: A verified downloadable certificate with QR verification.
